key_points: The story highlights the transformation of a dishonest beggar through compassion
Sergei attempts to reform Lushkoff through strict advice and work
Olga’s silent kindness and empathy deeply influence Lushkoff
Lushkoff gradually reforms and achieves dignity through honest work
The story shows how compassion can inspire moral change
characters: Lushkoff – Symbol of human weakness and eventual redemption
Sergei – Symbol of discipline and moral instruction
Olga – Symbol of empathy, compassion, and silent reform
themes: Dignity of labour as a path to self-respect
literary_devices: Irony – The real reformer is Olga, not Sergei as initially believed
Realism – The story portrays genuine social problems such as poverty and alcoholism
Character development – Lushkoff’s transformation drives the narrative
exam_tips: Discuss Olga as the moral centre of the story
Explain how compassion leads to reform
Analyse the contrast between Sergei’s strictness and Olga’s empathy
Focus on the theme of dignity of labour
